reasoning: NASA announced in early 2024 that the Artemis 3 mission (the next scheduled crewed lunar landing) is officially delayed from late 2025 to no earlier than September 2026. More recent sources from late 2025 indicate it has been pushed back even further to mid-2027. There are no other nations or entities (such as China or SpaceX) with credible, publicly announced plans or capabilities to complete a crewed lunar landing by the end of 2025. Therefore, it is virtually impossible for a human to step foot on the moon by December 31, 2025.
